author: @stampbou
text: Thank you for your video. I was able to make a coil  like yours and placed it on a nu wave pic pro inductive cooker and am heating my entire living space with 1,000 watts. It also produces an excess of dc current with additional smaller coils to produce lighting. I had to make special voltage regulators to get rid  of the noise created by the frequency of 22,000 off the coils used for dc stuff. See u tube Gegene for the videos I copied. Fantastic! Again, thank you.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: interesting work you are doing - i will have a look

replies.author: @stampbou
replies.text:
Robert Murray-Smith I placed a very small flat pancake coil on top of the larger one to glean excess dc power from my cook top. I hooked it to an led lamp I made for soldering.
The following Lamp consists of 10 Harbor Freight Flashlights removed from the case.
The led boards are super glued together.

They are powered by a 2" coil that sits on a hot plate. (Tesla bi-filar Coil  #16 solid wire.)
The coil produces ac current and is converted by a full wave bridge rectifier.  The positive
side of the rectifier is connected to all Input leads of the (5) voltage regulators.
 
The voltage at the hot plate is 6.0 volts. The light circuit consists of 5 separate (7805) 5 Volt
voltage regulators. Each regulator powers 2 (24 led) boards. Total 5X48 or 240 super bright
white leds. Each regulator circuit contains 3 capacitors. In 470uf , out 100uf + .01 uf.

If you would like photos give me your email addy.
Thanks Lynn
